channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: U.S. and Iran Exchange New Attacks
item.description: U.S. Central Command said it conducted a round of “self-defense strikes” on military targets in southern Iran over the weekend. Iran’s military later said it had targeted a base in retaliation.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times

item.title: Dozens Killed as Explosion Flattens Rebel-Held Village in Myanmar
item.description: The local authorities said the cause was an accident at a warehouse that held explosives for mining operations.
item.dc:creator: Sui-Lee Wee

media:content.media:description: The aftermath of an explosion on Sunday in Kaung Tup, a village in Myanmar, as seen in a handout photo provided by Palaung Land, a group with links to the rebel group that controls the area.

item.title: Far-Right Candidate Advances to Runoff in Colombia’s Presidential Election
item.description: Abelardo De La Espriella, a right-wing outsider, will face Iván Cepeda, a progressive senator, in Colombia’s presidential election runoff.
item.dc:creator: Julie Yoon

item.title: Después de la deportación: una familia de Colombia se despide del sueño americano
item.description: ¿Qué ocurre con los cientos de miles de personas que han sido enviadas de vuelta a los lugares que desesperadamente querían dejar atrás?
item.dc:creator: Juan Arredondo

media:content.media:credit: Juan Arredondo for The New York Times

item.title: Syrian Officers Who Hid in Plain Sight Face War Crime Charges in Austria
item.description: The trial, Austria’s first against Assad regime officials, will be a rare chance for Syrian witnesses to confront two men they have accused of torture.
item.dc:creator: Carlotta Gall
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
Commission for International Justice and Accountability

media:content.media:description: The burned remains of an office at the military intelligence detention facility in Raqqa in 2013, after rebels captured the city from Assad regime forces.

item.title: N.Y.C. Children May Get Up to $3,000 From City for College Accounts
item.description: The city’s current savings plan starts with $100. A City Council proposal would make the initial contributions among the highest anywhere.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Haag

media:content.media:description: Julie Menin, the speaker of the City Council, is proposing a substantial expansion of the city’s investment in college savings plans.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for June 1, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: World Cup 2026 Schedule: Where to Watch in the U.S., Canada and Mexico
item.description: The biggest party on earth is about to kick off. Here’s how to enjoy every thrilling minute.
item.dc:creator: Danial Adkison, Shannon Sims, Derek M. Norman, Gerald Narciso and Elisabeth Malkin

item.title: What to Watch For in the NY Primary: Key Races and Why They Matter
item.description: Congressional primaries in Manhattan, Brooklyn and beyond could test the strength of Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s socialist movement and reverberate across the country.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Fandos

media:content.media:credit: Victor J. Blue for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Competitive primary contests are taking place in six House districts in New York City and elsewhere in the state.

item.title: The Congressional Primary Tearing the Mamdani Coalition in Two
item.description: The Democratic battle over a House seat in New York City has reignited old tensions over gentrification, identity and who gets to define the left.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Fandos

media:content.media:credit: Adam Gray for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mr. Reynoso has received the backing of major labor unions.

item.title: U.S. Says It Hit More Military Targets in Southern Iran
item.description: The U.S. military said it carried out ‘self-defense strikes’ over the weekend. Iran’s military said it had targeted a military base in retaliation for an American strike.
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhuang
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: Fishing boats idle along the Strait of Hormuz at Qeshm Island, Iran, in May.

item.title: Oil Prices Jump as U.S. and Iran Exchange Fire
item.description: Oil prices climbed as investors weighed a renewed exchange of military strikes between the United States and Iran against signs that both sides remain engaged in negotiations.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Taiwan Criticizes China’s Expulsion of New York Times Reporter
item.description: The Taiwan president’s spokeswoman said China used “baseless pretexts” to punish journalists, which she called evidence that Beijing was a “troublemaker.”
item.dc:creator: David Pierson

media:content.media:description: Taiwan’s president, Lai Ching-te, in Taipei last month.

item.title: Las intervenciones internacionales de Trump se estancaron. Y eso le molesta
item.description: En Ucrania, en Gaza y ahora en Irán, las primeras declaraciones del presidente Donald Trump sobre victorias fáciles han dado paso a la dura realidad.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Uno de los ayudantes más cercanos de Trump dijo recientemente que destruir emplazamientos nucleares desde el aire es lo que Estados Unidos hace mejor, y que controlar los acontecimientos políticos en naciones como Irán, Rusia y Ucrania es lo que hace peor.

item.title: Word of the Day: recondite
item.description: This word has appeared in five articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Brooklyn Rivera, Nicaraguan Indigenous Leader, Dies in Government Custody
item.description: Brooklyn Rivera, 73, one of the most prominent Miskito political leaders in recent history, was arrested by the government in 2023. His family and other groups dispute the circumstances surrounding his death.
item.dc:creator: James Wagner

media:content.media:description: The Miskito rebel leader Brooklyn Rivera, right, speaking during a news conference in Managua, Nicaragua, as his fellow leader Steadman Fagoth Müller looked on, in 1988.

item.title: Nvidia Has a Plan to Put Its Chips in Personal Computers
item.description: The world’s most valuable company is chasing Intel and Apple as it tries to bring A.I. agents to laptops and desktops.
item.dc:creator: Tripp Mickle

media:content.media:description: Nvidia’s chief executive, Jensen Huang, in Taipei, Taiwan, last week. Nvidia is betting its computers will hit the market at the right time, just as A.I. agents are becoming more popular.

item.title: ¿Extraterrestres o demonios? La divulgación ovni divide a los cristianos conservadores de EE. UU.
item.description: Cuando el gobierno de Trump ordenó publicar archivos sobre vida extraterrestre, una red de pastores y presentadores de pódcast ya tenía su respuesta lista.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Graham

media:content.media:credit: Departamento de Defensa de EE. UU., vía Getty Images
media:content.media:description: El Pentágono publicó recientemente archivos sobre ovnis que incluían videos borrosos e imágenes fijas que no mostraban nada definitivo.

item.title: ‘Cape Fear’ and 7 More Shows to Watch on TV This Week
item.description: A new Apple TV series premieres, and the 79th Tony Awards ceremony will be broadcast live.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez

media:content.media:description: Patrick Wilson and Amy Adams in “Cape Fear.”

item.title: The Joy of Hate-Watching A.I. Slop
item.description: A.I. slop is reconstituting a shared cultural base line.
item.dc:creator: Dan Brooks

item.title: Germany Has Lost What It Did Best
item.description: The economy is in a terrible mess.
item.dc:creator: Konstantin Richter

item.title: ‘Euphoria’ Creator Sam Levinson Explains Why This Is The End
item.description: In an exclusive interview with Popcast, ‘Euphoria’ creator, Sam Levinson, explains why he felt it was time to bring the series to a close.

item.title: Sam Levinson on Why ‘Euphoria’ Had to End This Way. For Good.
item.description: In an exclusive interview, Sam Levinson explained why he felt it was time to bring the series to a close and responded to its detractors.
item.dc:creator: Joe Coscarelli, Jon Caramanica and Caroline Tompkins

item.title: ‘Euphoria’ Creator Sam Levinson Explains Why This Is The End
item.description: In an exclusive interview with Popcast, Sam Levinson explains why he felt it was time to bring the series to a close and responded to its detractors.

item.title: How an Act of Vandalism Helped a Dissident Find His Voice
item.description: Li Ying, known as Teacher Li to his 2.2 million followers on X, doesn’t live in China but still faces smear campaigns and death threats. He is not letting that stop him.
item.dc:creator: Li Yuan
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

item.title: No Corrections: June 1, 2026
item.description: No corrections appeared in print on Monday, June 1, 2026.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Garbage Trucks Have No Fuel, Worsening Cuba’s Trouble With Trash
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Monday, June 1, 2026.

item.title: ‘And Then the Rodeo Burned Down’ Review: Tenderness as Transgression
item.description: In their Off Broadway debut at Ars Nova, Xhloe and Natasha play two rodeo clowns, until the lights go out and the show takes a turn.
item.dc:creator: Elisabeth Vincentelli
item.category: And Then The Rodeo Burned Down (Play)

item.title: La otra crisis de Cuba: basura, dengue y chikunguña
item.description: El bloqueo petrolero del gobierno Trump dejó a La Habana sin combustible para recoger la basura, pero el problema tiene raíces más profundas: décadas de ineficiencia estatal.
item.dc:creator: Ed Augustin and Frances Robles

media:content.media:description: Basura en la calle en La Habana en diciembre.

item.title: 10-Minute Challenge: ‘The Painted Room’ by Lois Dodd
item.description: We’d like you to look at one piece of art for 10 minutes, uninterrupted.
item.dc:creator: Larry Buchanan

item.title: 2025 Wildfires Were the Costliest Ever, Researchers Say
item.description: Severe, hard-to-control blazes in densely populated areas like Los Angeles drove the year’s record losses.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Dzombak

media:content.media:credit: Loren Elliott for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Pacific Palisades, Calif., on Jan. 7, 2025. Fires in the Los Angeles area last year burned least 90 square miles, killing at least 31 people and displacing more than 150,000.

item.title: Mapa: un terremoto de magnitud 6,0 se registra en la costa de Chile
item.description: Mira la localización del epicentro del sismo y el área de movimiento.
item.dc:creator: William B. Davis, Madison Dong, Judson Jones, John Keefe, Joey K. Lee, Bea Malsky and Víctor Hernández Marroquín

item.title: Brote de ébola: así es el epicentro en República Democrática del Congo
item.description: A remote gold mining town is under siege, as medical workers struggle to beat back a surge of deaths and infections.
item.dc:creator: Declan Walsh and Arlette Bashizi

media:content.media:credit: Arlette Bashizi for The New York Times

item.title: Map: 6.0-Magnitude Earthquake Strikes off the Coast of Chile
item.description: View the location of the quake’s epicenter and shake area.
item.dc:creator: William B. Davis, Madison Dong, Judson Jones, John Keefe, Joey K. Lee and Bea Malsky

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for June 1, 2026
item.description: Kenneth Cortes does the crossword proud.
item.dc:creator: Sam Corbin

item.title: Notre Dame Students Abused by Priest in ‘Weighing Scheme,’ Report Finds
item.description: An investigation commissioned by Notre Dame found that the Catholic university could have done more to respond to allegations of sexual misconduct.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Saul

media:content.media:credit: Evan Jenkins for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A new report details allegations of sexual abuse at the University of Notre Dame.

item.title: Havoc in Hawaii’s Coffee Fields After an Earthquake Destroyed Water Collection Tanks
item.description: An earthquake destroyed many water catchment systems on Hawaii’s Big Island, capping a spring filled with devastating floods.
item.dc:creator: Libby Leonard and Vivian Yee

media:content.media:credit: Megan Spelman for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Roy Santana, 80, in front of his water catchment tank, which broke during a 6.0 earthquake earlier this month.

item.title: China Exports Surveillance
item.description: The country has spent decades perfecting a surveillance state at home. Now it’s promoting its ideology of state control, and the technology to enforce it, abroad.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold

media:content.media:credit: Adam Ferguson for The New York Times

item.title: U.S. Military Is Quietly Guiding Ships Through the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: U.S. Central Command has helped around 70 commercial ships pass through the strait in the last three weeks, an official said.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: Vessels waiting to pass through the Strait of Hormuz. Before the U.S.-Israeli attacks on Iran, well over 100 commercial ships a day passed through the strait.

item.title: Another Night of Violent Protests Outside a Newark ICE Detention Center
item.description: Protesters and the police clashed again outside of an ICE detention center in New Jersey on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Cynthia Silva

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Alban for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Demonstrators and police officers outside the Delaney Hall immigration detention center in Newark on Saturday.

item.title: Nearly 800 Arrested in France After Champions League Final, French Officials Said
item.description: Nearly 800 people were arrested in France after Paris St.-Germain won the Champions League on Saturday, French officials said. Thousands in Paris took to the streets to celebrate, leading to some violent clashes with police officers.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Mitssunaga

item.title: Trump Hits the Stalemate Phase of His Interventions in Gaza, Ukraine and Now Iran
item.description: In Ukraine, Gaza and now Iran, President Trump’s early declarations of easy wins have given way to harsh reality.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: One of President Trump’s close aides said recently that destroying nuclear sites from the air is what America does best, and that controlling political events in nations like Iran, Russia and Ukraine is what the United States does worst.

item.title: As Democrats Worry About Senate Race, Platner Attacks Reports About Sexual Messages
item.description: Graham Platner, whose contest in Maine is a key to Democrats’ hopes of winning the Senate, sought to discredit reports that he had exchanged sexual messages with women outside his marriage.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Lerer, Tim Balk and Katie Glueck

media:content.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Graham Platner, a Democratic candidate for Senate in Maine, is trying to unseat Senator Susan Collins, a Republican.

item.title: Israel Captures Beaufort Castle as It Advances Into Lebanon
item.description: The seizure of Beaufort Castle evoked bitter memories, in both countries, of the deadly fighting there during Israel’s nearly two-decade occupation of southern Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: McKinnon de Kuyper

media:content.media:description: A view of the Beaufort Castle fortification atop a cliff above the Litani River in southern Lebanon, last year.

item.title: Release of Tina Peters, Election Denier and Former County Clerk, Divides Colorado City
item.description: The former clerk of Mesa County is scheduled to be set free on Monday, after her nine-year prison sentence was commuted by Gov. Jared Polis.
item.dc:creator: Jack Healy

media:content.media:description: Tina Peters, a county clerk and election denier who was imprisoned for tampering with voting machines, in 2022.

item.title: Vasana Montgomery, ‘Love Island USA’ Contestant, Dismissed After Using Racial Slur
item.description: Last season, two contestants were also kicked off the reality show when videos showing them using racial slurs surfaced during filming.
item.dc:creator: Derrick Bryson Taylor

media:content.media:description: Two videos of Vasana Montgomery, 25, using a racial slur surfaced on social media last week.

item.title: Young Moviegoers Power ‘Backrooms’ to $82 Million in Ticket Sales
item.description: The horror film, which cost $10 million and came from a 20-year-old filmmaker, added to evidence that young people will go to theaters for the right offering.
item.dc:creator: Brooks Barnes

item.title: Trump Is Dominating GOP Primary Elections. Does That Mean Victory in the Midterms?
item.description: Come November, the Republican Party will need the support of voters outside of President Trump’s base, many of whom are deeply dissatisfied with the economy and the Iran war.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs
item.category: Kennedy, John F, Center for the Performing Arts

media:content.media:credit: Al Drago for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump heading to Trump National Golf Club in Sterling, Va., on Saturday. Mr. Trump’s preferred candidates have had strong showings in Republican primaries this year.

item.title: Timeline: Where and When Trump Has Involved the U.S. Military in International Conflicts
item.description: The United States has been involved in many military operations around the world since President Trump returned to office.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Members of the military transferring the remains of two Iowa National Guard members killed in Syria at Dover Air Force Base in Delaware last year.

item.title: ‘Othello’ Review: Wendell Pierce Steps Into Iago’s Twisted Web
item.description: This production in the nation’s capital, with an enticingly opaque Iago, attempts to make Shakespeare’s tragedy relevant to our age of conspiracies.
item.dc:creator: Rhoda Feng
